conditions: Chronic back pain (lumbar degenerative disc disease)
medications: Cyclobenzaprine 5 mg at bedtime as needed
Melatonin 5 mg at bedtime as needed
health.mental_health: Ongoing financial stress after bankruptcy with periods of anxiety and sleep disruption; he avoids formal therapy due to cost and stigma but uses brief breathing exercises and tries to walk when his back allows.
recent_visits: Primary care follow-up for blood pressure and medication refills (January)
Physical therapy re-evaluation for low back pain and home exercise plan (February)
Orthopedics consult to discuss imaging and non-surgical options for lumbar pain (December)
Dental cleaning postponed twice due to budget; rescheduled for next month

finance.income_range: $70,000–$90,000 base salary; variable bonus up to ~$5,000 depending on quarterly performance
finance.debt: Filed Chapter 7 bankruptcy last year after a combination of medical bills, high-interest credit cards, and a short period of reduced hours; now rebuilding credit with a secured card and a strict repayment plan for remaining non-discharged obligations (a small IRS payment plan and a car loan).
investments: Small emergency savings in a high-yield savings account
recent_transactions: Monthly payment to IRS installment plan
Secured credit card payment (kept under 10% utilization)
Groceries at Giant and Costco (bulk to save money)

relationships: Partner: Daniel Cho (together 6 years; finances are a recurring tension point since the bankruptcy)
Sister: Marisol Rivera (in Silver Spring; occasional support and blunt advice about budgeting)
Friend: Kevin O'Neal (former coworker; checks in about job openings and side gigs)
Older neighbor: Mrs. Irene Watkins (helps by receiving packages when Liam works late)
social.social_media_activity: Low-volume Facebook and Instagram use; mostly restaurant promos, occasional photos from the National Mall walks, and quiet engagement with neighborhood groups; he avoids posting about money or health issues.
private_conversations: Discussing the bankruptcy details and whether to disclose it to Daniel’s family
Arguments about shared expenses, rent splits, and whether to move to a cheaper area
Talking with Marisol about a possible second job and fear of another debt spiral
Confiding in Kevin about wage negotiations and embarrassment about credit score

schedule.work_hours: Typically 50–60 hours/week; Tue–Sat split shifts (10:30am–3:00pm, 5:00pm–10:30pm) with one admin-heavy day; on-call for staffing issues and vendor deliveries.
regular_appointments: Physical therapy exercises at home (20 minutes, Mon/Wed/Fri mornings)
Blood pressure check at a pharmacy kiosk every other Sunday
Monthly budgeting session using a spreadsheet and credit monitoring (first Monday evening)
upcoming_events: Physical therapy follow-up appointment (next Wednesday at 8:00am)
Meeting with landlord to discuss lease renewal and possible rent increase (in two weeks)
Restaurant health inspection window expected this month (unannounced)
Dinner with Daniel’s parents in Arlington (next Saturday evening)
schedule.daily_routine: Up early to stretch and manage back pain, coffee and light breakfast, commute by Metro when possible to avoid parking costs, long hours on the floor and in the office, late-night wind-down with a heating pad and brief budgeting check-ins.

professional.company: Harbor & Pine Kitchen (mid-priced American bistro near Capitol Hill; part of a small local restaurant group)
projects: Reducing food waste and tightening inventory controls to improve margins
Cross-training hosts and servers to stabilize scheduling
Renegotiating linen and produce vendor contracts
Implementing a new POS update and staff training sessions
professional.performance_issues: Occasional fatigue and irritability during double shifts due to insomnia and back pain; he is highly motivated to keep the job stable after bankruptcy and sometimes overworks, leading to minor mistakes in end-of-day cash reconciliation that he double-checks to avoid any suspicion.
workplace_relationships: Strong working relationship with Executive Chef Tasha Nguyen, but tension over labor costs and menu changes
Mentors an assistant manager, Julio Martinez, who wants a promotion and pushes for more authority
Ongoing conflict with one bartender over shift assignments and tip-out policies
Frequent coordination with the owner, Samir Patel, who is supportive but demanding about KPIs

hobbies: Walking the National Mall and Rock Creek Park when his back allows
Cooking at home with leftovers and budget-friendly meal prep
Watching baseball and listening to sports radio
Thrift-store browsing and Facebook Marketplace for household items
Podcasts about personal finance and restaurant management
habits: Uses a heating pad nightly and does morning stretching to manage pain
Tracks every purchase in a budgeting app and checks credit score weekly
Limits alcohol to 1–2 drinks/week after noticing it worsens sleep and reflux
Picks up occasional paid catering shifts for extra cash
Avoids rideshares and drives only when necessary to save money
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a small one-bedroom apartment in Northeast D.C.; considering downsizing or moving across the line into Hyattsville if rent rises. Daniel stays over several nights a week but they are not fully cohabitating due to financial boundaries post-bankruptcy.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Tries to follow a lower-sodium diet for blood pressure; avoids spicy and acidic foods when GERD flares; relies on simple, high-protein meals and brings staff meal leftovers home to cut grocery costs.
